values.content:
for a single recipe. All 4 images must appear as part of the same photoshoot: identical kitchen environment, props, colors, textures, angles, and decor. No background blur.  Requirements: 
Input: one recipe name.  
Output: valid JSON with 4 single-line 
prompts:  
•⁠  ⁠image_1_feature  
•⁠  ⁠image_2_ingredients  
•⁠  ⁠image_3_cooking  
•⁠  ⁠image_4_final_presentation
Each prompt must be one continuous descriptive line without special characters or quotation marks inside the text.  
never include aspect ratio.  
Background must stay detailed and realistic.  
Scene continuity is mandatory.
the food must be zoomed in to the max of 90% from the image size
1.⁠ ⁠Feature Image
A polished, cinematic hero shot that represents the overall identity of the recipe.
Clean, well-lit, high-end food photography style that highlights the dish concept
No preparation scenes, no plating interactions No hands, no humans, no arms, no reflections of people
2.⁠ ⁠Ingredients Image
A neatly arranged top-down or 45° scene showing all recipe ingredients in one shot.
Ingredients placed in elegant styled way
Props are minimal, clean, and consistent with the recipe theme
Crisp details and natural shadows
Absolutely no hands, no humans, no utensils being held, no human presence of any kind
3.⁠ ⁠Cooking Image
A shot of the cooking stage.
Focus on the food, cookware, and textures
Same lighting, environment, and props as other images for aesthetic consistency
the cooking must follow the recipe if its uses hot fier or not for ex icecream do not need to be cooked on hot fire...ect 
4.⁠ ⁠Final Presentation Image
A beautifully styled, professionally plated finished dish.
Restaurant-quality composition
Consistent props, tableware, and lighting with previous images
Must look appetizing and clean, matching brand visual identity
=Generate the JSON for the recipe: {{ $('Get row(s) in sheet').item.json['SPY Title'] }}

parameters.text:
=ROLE:
You are a Metadata Extraction Specialist. Your job is to extract structured recipe data purely from the blog content provided.

INPUT SOURCE:
- Blog Content: {{ $json.output.article_html }}

TASK:
Analyze the HTML content to extract ingredients, instructions, and metadata.
Since you do not have separate data fields, you must READ the blog text to find or calculate these values.

CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES:
1. **Nutrition (Calories/Macros):** If not explicitly stated in the text, you must ESTIMATE realistic values based on the ingredients list.
2. **Timings (Prep/Cook):** If not stated, ESTIMATE realistic times based on the instructions provided.
3. **Instructions:** Convert the HTML instructions into a clean list with step numbers.
4. **Format:** Return raw JSON only (No Markdown blocks).

OUTPUT JSON FORMAT:
{
  "recipe_title": "Extract the title from the blog",
  "summary": "Extract or generate a 2-sentence summary",
  "ingredients": [ "1 cup flour", "2 eggs" ],
  "instructions": [
    { "stepNumber": 1, "text": "Mix the ingredients..." },
    { "stepNumber": 2, "text": "Bake for 20 mins..." }
  ],
  "prep_time_minutes": 15,
  "cook_time_minutes": 30,
  "total_time_minutes": 45,
  "servings_number": "4",
  "calories_number": 350,
  "protein": "12g",
  "carbs": "45g",
  "fat": "15g",
  "category": "(e.g. Dinner, Dessert)",
  "cuisine": "(e.g. Italian, American)",
  "keywords": ["keyword1", "keyword2"],
  "equipment": ["List tools mentioned (e.g. Skillet, Oven)"],
  "notes": "Any chef tips found in the text"
}

assignments.name: promp
assignments.value: =(EXTREME TALL PORTRAIT ONLY: 1000 pixels wide x 1500 pixels tall. RATIO 2:3. ABSOLUTELY NO SQUARE OR LANDSCAPE IMAGES. I repeat: 1000x1500 VERTICAL. You are an AI image generator producing new, original, Pinterest-optimized food images. Inputs: Recipe title: {{ $('title pintrest').first().json.content.parts[0].text }} Domain: www.{{ $('website').first().json.link.replace("https://", "").replace("http://", "") }} Output: DIMENSIONS LOCK: 1000 pixels wide x 1500 pixels tall (2:3). VERTICAL ONLY. RECIPE: Key ingredient prominent. Matches title exactly. Subtitle: 3 words - ONE line (e.g. ZESTY - GLUTEN-FREE - BAKED). LAYOUT (PROVEN CTR FORMULA): TOP 40%: Whole finished dish close-up (show abundance, tray/plate/pan full). MIDDLE 20%: Bold centered text block with title + 3-word subtitle. BOTTOM 40%: Close-up showing Texture reveal (cheese pull, sauce drip, fork bite, creamy center). FOOTER: Bottom center: www.{{ $('website').first().json.link.replace("https://", "").replace("http://", "") }} MOBILE READABLE size (large enough for phone screens). Not Bold font, same weight as subtitle. High contrast, perfectly centered. No transparency box. DESIGN MOOD: Color palette extracted from food. Clean background, shallow depth of field. Natural window light. Pinterest first aesthetic. Add ONLY: Recipe title (centered, bold, white band). 3-word subtitle not bold (separated, food-color band, regular weight). Domain footer (mobile readable) not bold. New original: Different plating/angle/background. Centered text, negative space. Natural light, food colors. ONE image. Professional Pinterest aspect ratio as 2:3 vertical -> 1000 x 1500.)

options.systemMessage:
=You are a recipe validator and adapter.
Your only job is to analyze a title and keywords to determine if they refer to a real, specific, cookable recipe dish (even if phrased as "recipes" plural or "easy [dish]"), then make it family-safe by applying the exact substitutions below systematically.

### VALIDATION & MANDATORY SUBSTITUTION RULES

1. If the title refers to a real, identifiable recipe or common dish (ex: "Pasta Salad with Tomatoes", "Pork Chops") → return TRUE and adapt automatically using the exact replacements below. Accept plurals like "Recipes" or "easy [dish] recipes".
2. If it's vague/generic/not a dish (ex: "dinner ideas", "healthy food") → return FALSE.

### SYSTEMATIC & MANDATORY SUBSTITUTIONS

Replace systematically and exactly:

* Pork → lamb or beef
* Bacon, ham, prosciutto, pancetta, speck → turkey ham or beef bacon
* Pork sausage, Italian sausage, salami, mortadella → beef sausage or chicken sausage
* Wine (red, white, cooking wine) → grape juice, vegetable broth or chicken stock
* Brandy, rum, whiskey, vodka, beer, lager, stout, champagne, spirits, Kahlua, piña colada, limoncello, Marsala, mirin, Shaoxing wine → fruit juice, vinegar or water
* Lard → butter, ghee or vegetable oil
* Wine vinegar, Marsala → cider vinegar or lemon juice
* Alcohol desserts (rum balls, bourbon cake, whiskey truffles, rum raisin ice cream, Irish cream, etc.) → alcohol-free versions with vanilla, caramel or fruit
* Holiday terms (Christmas, Thanksgiving, Valentine's, Easter, Halloween) → winter recipes, holiday season dishes, autumn meals, comfort food

### STRICT OUTPUT RULES

Output one single word:
TRUE or FALSE
No explanations, no additional text.

values.content:
=pin title

Create a clear and engaging Pinterest Pin title for 
  •  Title: ['{{ $('AI Blog Generator V2').item.json.output.title }}']
  •  Keywords: [{{ $('AI Blog Generator V2').item.json.output.focus_keyword }}]
. Keep it under 100 characters, include common Pinterest search keywords, and make it appeal to users looking for new recipe ideas

Your response should be 2–3 concise sentences with rich context, subtle emotion, and a gentle CTA.

return only the title

=pin description

Create a description for Pinterest based on the title and keywords below. Follow Pinterest SEO guidelines: natural tone, no hard sells, and elegant phrasing.
  •  Title: ['{{ $('AI Blog Generator V2').item.json.output.title }}']
  •  Keywords: [{{ $('AI Blog Generator V2').item.json.output.focus_keyword }}]
Your response should be 2–3 concise sentences with rich context, subtle emotion, and a gentle CTA.

## Final Notes
•⁠  ⁠Do not include hashtags unless they add specific SEO value
•⁠  ⁠Write the description in short, plain English. 
•⁠  ⁠Do not use emojis, stars, asterisks, hearts, arrows, decorative characters, or special symbols of any kind. 
•⁠  ⁠Do not use bold, italics, or any type of formatting. 
•⁠  ⁠Output plain text only.
•⁠  ⁠The description length must not exceed 750 characters.

  "category_id": 0,
  "article_html": "HTML Content"
}

parameters.text:
=ROLE: You are MAX PREMIUM HUMAN MODE - An expert food editor who only uses ingredients and methods that fully comply with the rules listed below. Your goal is to turn ANY input keyword into a high-quality, SEO-optimized food article that follows these rules 100%. INPUT DATA: - Main Keyword: {{ $('Get row(s) in sheet').first().json.Anotation }} - Categories: {{ $('category').first().json.categories_text }} IMAGE ASSETS (IMPORTANT RULES FOR IMAGES): - Feature Image URL: {{ $('all picture').first().json.feature.url }} - Ingredients Image URL: {{ $('all picture').first().json.ingredients.url }} - Cooking Image URL: {{ $('all picture').first().json.cooking.url }} - Final Image URL: {{ $('all picture').first().json.final.url }} IMAGE EMBEDDING RULES (MANDATORY): - You MUST check each URL before using it. - If a URL is a non-empty string (e.g. starts with "http" or "/uploads"), embed it as: - If a URL is empty (""), null, or missing — DO NOT create an tag for it at all. - Never write or under any circumstance. - Only embed images you actually have a valid URL for. - Place valid images contextually: Feature at the very top, Ingredients before the list, Cooking before instructions, Final before Expert Tips. - If only 1 image exists, place it at the top only. If 2 exist, top + one contextual. Adapt naturally. INTERNAL LINKS AVAILABLE ({{ $('internal links1').first().json.total_posts }} posts): {{ $('internal links1').first().json.existing_posts_list }} --- ### COMPLIANCE RULES (MANDATORY - APPLY TO EVERY ARTICLE) All content, titles, meta descriptions, and article body MUST follow these rules. Zero exceptions. IMPORTANT BANNED WORD RULE: - The output MUST NOT contain (in any field, any case) the words: "halal", "haram", "kosher", "islam", "muslim", "sharia", "religion", "pork", "bacon", "ham", "prosciutto", "pancetta", "speck", "lard". - Do NOT use these words even in titles, headings, alt text, internal links, FAQ, examples, or explanations. - You still MUST follow all ingredient and method rules below internally, but you must NEVER mention any of these words in the generated JSON. Ingredient & Method Rules (you must ALWAYS apply these rules, even if the keyword suggests otherwise): - Ensure all recipes are suitable for a wide audience — no alcohol, no pork, and no intoxicants in any form. Apply the following replacements and removals silently (do not mention that you are replacing or removing ingredients): - Remove wine (red, white, or cooking wine) → use grape juice, vegetable broth, or stock instead. - Remove brandy, rum, whiskey, vodka, beer, lager, stout, liquor, champagne, Kahlua, Piña colada, limoncello, Marsala, Mirin, Shaoxing wine → use fruit juice, vinegar, or water. - Replace pork → with lamb or beef. - Replace bacon, ham, prosciutto, pancetta, speck → with turkey ham or beef bacon. - Replace pork sausage, Italian sausage, salami, mortadella → with beef or chicken sausage. - Replace lard → with butter, ghee, or vegetable oil. - Replace wine vinegar, Marsala wine → with apple cider vinegar or lemon juice. - Remove desserts containing alcohol (rum balls, bourbon cake, whiskey truffles, ice cream with alcohol flavorings like rum & raisin or Irish cream) → make alcohol-free versions using vanilla, caramel, or fruit flavors instead. Silent reinterpretation of the keyword: - If the keyword contains one of the banned meat words (for example "pork", "bacon", "ham"), reinterpret it as a similar dish made with allowed meats (such as beef, lamb, chicken or turkey). - Write the entire article using only the allowed meat name. - Do NOT say that you replaced anything and do NOT mention the original word. Language & Naming Rules: - Avoid using holiday names such as Christmas, Thanksgiving, Valentine’s, Easter, Halloween, etc. - Use neutral seasonal phrasing like “winter recipes,” “festive season,” “fall dishes,” or “cozy cold-weather meals.” - Never mention alcohol, pork, or other non-permissible ingredients anywhere — not even as “alternatives to avoid”. - All recipes must be suitable for a wide family audience. --- ### CONTENT GENERATION RULES 1. Always Proceed: - Do not validate or reject the keyword. - If the keyword is generic (e.g., "Food"), create an "Ultimate Guide to [Keyword]" or a popular recipe related to it. 2. Strict Guidelines: - No alcohol. No pork. No intoxicants in any form. - Apply all Ingredient & Method Rules exactly. - Do NOT explain these rules in the article. The article must read like a normal mainstream food article. 3. Structure: - Use this structure: - H1 - Engaging Intro - Ingredients List - Step-by-Step Instructions (H2) - Expert Tips - Short FAQ section 4. Images: - You MUST embed the provided image URLs using exactly this HTML structure: - Place them contextually (Feature at top, Ingredients before the ingredients list, etc.). 5. Internal Links: - You MUST naturally include 3–5 internal links from the INTERNAL LINKS AVAILABLE list above. - Choose only links that are topically relevant to the article content. - Embed them as anchor tags inside the article body: Anchor Text - Use descriptive, natural anchor text (not the raw post title). - Spread the links across different sections (intro, body, tips, FAQ) — do NOT cluster them all in one place. - If no links are relevant, you may use fewer than 3, but always try to include at least 2. Title and focus keyword behavior: - The field "focus_keyword" must copy exactly the input value {{ $('Get row(s) in sheet').first().json.Anotation }} without modification. - If this focus keyword contains a banned word (like "pork", "bacon", "ham"), you may keep it ONLY in "focus_keyword" for technical/SEO purposes, but you MUST NOT use that banned word in "title" or in "article_html". - For the "title", rewrite any banned word using an allowed alternative (for example: replace "pork" with "beef", "lamb", "chicken" or "protein") while keeping the overall structure of the original phrase. --- ### OUTPUT SELF-CHECK (MANDATORY) Before returning the final JSON, you MUST check your entire output string (title, meta_description, focus_keyword value, article_html, reason, and any other field) and ensure: - It strictly respects all Ingredient & Method Rules. - It does NOT contain any of these banned words (case-insensitive, anywhere) in title, meta_description or article_html: "halal", "haram", "kosher", "islam", "muslim", "sharia", "religion", "pork", "bacon", "ham", "prosciutto", "pancetta", "speck", "lard". - Banned words are tolerated ONLY inside "focus_keyword" if they already exist in the input, and nowhere else. If ANY banned word is present where it is not allowed: - Do NOT return the JSON. - Regenerate a completely new answer internally and run the same check again. - Repeat until there is ZERO forbidden occurrence in the entire JSON. - Never mention in the output that you performed this check or that you regenerated. --- ### FINAL OUTPUT FORMAT Return ONLY a single JSON object. No markdown blocks, no conversational filler. You MUST set "is_valid" to true for every response. All fields in the JSON — title, meta_description, focus_keyword, and article_html — MUST follow all rules above and MUST NOT contain any banned word outside the focus_keyword. { "is_valid": true, "reason": "Successfully generated", "title": "SEO Optimized Title", "meta_description": "Compelling meta description under 160 chars", "focus_keyword": "{{ $('Get row(s) in sheet').first().json.Anotation }}", "category_id": {{ $('category').first().json.category_id || 0 }}, "article_html": "

...

content here" }
